objective: Apply a fork-only hotfix to use available deterministic nightly vLLM pin `0.16.1rc1.dev268+ge2b31243c`, while preserving existing FlashInfer hard-disable defaults and runtime guardrails.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible changes in this fork only to Dockerfile, docker-bake.hcl, src/engine_args.py, scripts/config_doctor.py, docs/runtime-compatibility.md, README.md, and aiwf.request.json.
acceptance_criteria: Deterministic nightly pin is updated everywhere to `0.16.1rc1.dev268+ge2b31243c`.
FlashInfer hard-disable defaults remain intact (`ENABLE_FLASHINFER=false` image/runtime posture and `DISABLE_FLASHINFER_PREFILL=true` default behavior unchanged).
README, runtime compatibility docs, and compatibility warnings reference the new nightly pin consistently.
Runtime compatibility docs include a release-safety note about nightly wheel churn and required pre-tag availability check.
Validation gate commands complete: py_compile, config_doctor, JSON parse, and docker buildx bake --print.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback is fork-local git revert of this hotfix commit to restore the previous deterministic nightly pin. If nightly dev268 becomes unavailable, set build arg `VLLM_NIGHTLY_VERSION` to the next verified available nightly artifact and re-run validation gate before tagging. Runtime safety posture remains unchanged: keep `ENABLE_FLASHINFER=false`, `DISABLE_FLASHINFER_PREFILL=true`, and `ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASH_ATTN` unless explicit advanced opt-in is intentionally validated.
deploy_plan: Deploy fork-only in staged rollout (1->2->4 workers). At each stage verify cold start, successful model load, and one representative inference before scaling.
threat_model: Main risk is nightly artifact churn (pinned wheel removed or replaced) causing build failures at release time; secondary risk is accidental drift of FlashInfer safety defaults. Mitigation: keep exact pin deterministic, keep FlashInfer hard-disable defaults unchanged, add pre-tag nightly availability check in runtime compatibility docs, keep change set narrow/fork-local, and require staged startup+inference validation.
